skill.name: decision-moment-skill
skill.description: Map and optimize decision moments across Awareness, Consideration, Conversion, and Retention, then attach specific assets, visuals, and automations to each stage. Use when designing funnels, campaigns, or retention systems.
skill.summary: Create structured decision-moment maps for funnels, campaigns, onboarding, and retention systems.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against the only skill file, SKILL.md. The flagged external command, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance patterns are false positives from Markdown table names, funnel-stage text, and ordinary marketing terms. No executable code, prompt injection, network behavior, credential access, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Text
low_findings.description: The static external command findings are Markdown inline code references to an asset location and database table names. They do not execute shell commands or instruct the model to run commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain Markdown references to social assets and table names only. No shell syntax, command invocation, or user-controlled execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern In Description
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding on the description line is caused by normal words in the skill description. The line describes funnel stages and does not mention or implement cryptographic behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a YAML description about journey mapping. There is no code, algorithm name, hashing operation, or encryption use on this line.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern In Use Case Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding in the usage section is plain text about designing onboarding journeys. It does not define, call, or recommend a cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a bullet point describing when to use the skill. No cryptographic primitive or security-sensitive operation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern In Asset List
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding in the consideration stage is ordinary marketing guidance about case studies and how-to videos. It is not security code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line lists recommended content asset types. There is no executable code or cryptographic implementation context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Pattern In Heading
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance finding is a journey-stage heading named Awareness. It does not contain scanning, probing, host discovery, or network instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a Markdown heading for a funnel stage. No network target, tool, port, protocol, or reconnaissance workflow is present.

content.value_statement: Teams often know their offer but cannot identify the exact message each buyer needs next. This skill turns offers into stage-based decision maps with matching assets, proof, channels, and follow-up actions.
actual_capabilities: Maps buyer stages from Awareness through Retention.
Defines decision moments with objections, proof needs, and asset types.
Connects assets to channels such as email, ads, social posts, and landing pages.
Creates execution maps for campaigns, onboarding flows, and retention systems.
Aligns recommendations with verified value propositions and existing assets.
limitations: Does not create final creative assets by itself.
Requires accurate offer, audience, and proof inputs from the user.
Does not validate conversion claims without supporting evidence.
Does not connect directly to databases, CRMs, or automation platforms.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Campaign Funnel
use_cases.description: Map each buyer stage, define the key objection, and choose the right asset for each channel.

use_cases.title: Improve an Existing Conversion Flow
use_cases.description: Find missing proof, unclear offers, and weak transition points across landing pages and follow-up messages.

use_cases.title: Design Customer Onboarding Moments
use_cases.description: Build a retention map that guides new customers from purchase to activation, success, and expansion.

prompt_templates.title: Map a Simple Offer
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the decision moment skill to map this offer: [offer]. Audience: [audience]. Include stages, buyer questions, objections, proof, and recommended asset types.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Funnel
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this funnel using the decision moment framework: [funnel summary]. Identify missing decision moments, weak proof, and the next asset to create.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Channel Execution Map
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an execution map for this campaign: [campaign goal]. Assign each decision moment to email, ads, social, landing pages, or onboarding touchpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Retention and Expansion
prompt_templates.prompt: Map retention decision moments for these customers: [customer segment]. Include activation signals, success proof, expansion triggers, and recommended follow-up assets.

output_examples.input: A consulting firm wants to sell a strategy workshop to operations leaders.
output: Awareness: define the operational bottleneck with a short educational post.
Consideration: answer solution doubts with a comparison guide and case study.
Conversion: remove final risk with a workshop agenda, testimonial, and clear booking page.

output_examples.input: A SaaS team has high trial signups but weak activation.
output: Problem moment: users do not understand the first valuable action.
Required proof: show the fastest path to a measurable result.
Recommended asset: onboarding email, in-app checklist, and short tutorial video.

output_examples.input: A customer success team wants to increase renewals.
output: Retention moment: confirm value before renewal discussions begin.
Blocking objection: customers may not see progress clearly.
Recommended asset: quarterly value recap with outcomes, next steps, and expansion options.

best_practices: Provide the audience, offer, proof points, and current assets before asking for a map.
Separate buyer objections by journey stage instead of using one generic objection list.
Tie every recommended asset to a clear buyer question or decision barrier.
anti_patterns: Using the skill to invent proof that has not been verified.
Skipping retention moments after the purchase stage.
Requesting a full funnel map without defining the target audience or offer.

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces decision-moment maps, asset recommendations, and execution maps for buyer journeys and retention flows.

faq.question: Can it create the final campaign assets?
faq.answer: It can recommend asset types and content direction, but final copy, design, and production require separate work.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Use a clear offer, target audience, value proposition, known objections, proof points, and existing funnel assets.

faq.question: Is this only for marketing teams?
faq.answer: No. Sales, product, and customer success teams can use it to map key decisions across the customer lifecycle.

faq.question: Does it require external tools?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a planning framework and only requests read and write access for local work.

faq.question: How does it handle unverified claims?
faq.answer: It instructs users to align recommendations with verified value propositions and avoid unsupported proof.
